nativeRange.habitat: Reef-associated; tends to occupy holes and crevices of seaward reefs from the surge zone to depths of around 26 m

diet.notes: Generally feeds on meaty foods such as silversides, krill, shrimp, and crustacean flesh; typically offered 2–3 substantial meals per week, delivered on a feeding stick due to poor eyesight and powerful jaws.

summary: A small, secretive reef moray from the Indo-Pacific generally reaching around 30 cm; tends to hide among live rock during the day and is generally considered semi-aggressive toward small fish and crustaceans.
careNotes: Also sold in the trade as 'Golden Dwarf Moray Eel', a name referring to the species' characteristic whitish-yellow to bright golden body coloration; this is not a distinct morph but simply an alternate trade name for the same species. Gymnothorax melatremus is commonly known as the Golden Dwarf Moray Eel, noted for its whitish-yellow to bright golden coloration, variable brown spotting, and distinctive faint pale-blue 'cat eyes'. It tends to be primarily nocturnal, hiding in caves and crevices during the day. This species is widely considered an escape specialist — a tightly-fitting, gap-free aquarium cover is generally regarded as essential. Feeding is best done with a feeding stick rather than by hand, given its poor eyesight and sharp teeth. Strong biological filtration and a protein skimmer are generally advised due to its carnivorous diet producing significant waste. LiveAquaria lists it as 'Easy' (care level), while ReefApp describes it as 'Hardy / suitable with care'; the intermediate designation here reflects the specialized tank requirements (escape-proofing, feeding method, heavy filtration) that go beyond typical beginner setups. Temperature disagreement noted between sources: LiveAquaria reports 72–78°F (22.2–25.6°C) and FishBase environmental modeling reports 25.4–28.9°C; the filed range (23.8–27.3°C) is the average of both. Minimum tank size averaged from LiveAquaria's 65 gal (246 L) and ReefApp's 300 L, yielding ~265 L. The species is reported to be solitary and secretive in the wild, with conspecific aggression commonly observed.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Cross-checked adult size (30 cm confirmed), native range, taxonomy (Muraenidae, Anguilliformes), and temperature range. FishBase reports environmental temperature of 25.4–28.9°C vs LiveAquaria's 22.2–25.6°C; filed range is the average.

additional.notes: Cross-checked adult size (30 cm confirmed), minimum tank size (300 L), native range (Indo-Pacific: East Africa to Marquesas). Listed salinity as ~1.026 SG standard.
